incidents.vector: Unspecified intrusion into ADT corporate systems; criminals later claimed to hold the data and threatened to publish it absent a ransom.
incidents.impact: Names, phone numbers, addresses, dates of birth, last four of Social Security numbers and tax IDs taken; criminals claim 10 million records. ADT says no payment data and no customer alarm systems were affected; identity protection offered to those notified.

incidents.victim: FinTech and cryptocurrency firms with macOS-using executives
incidents.vector: Operators contact targets on Telegram (often via a hijacked colleague's account) and lure them to a fake Zoom/Teams/Meet meeting where the user is asked to paste a 'fix' command or open a Zoom SDK update file, triggering a multi-stage AppleScript chain that installs persistence and a 'macrasv2' stealer.
incidents.impact: Successful intrusions yield browser sessions, stored credentials, macOS Keychain entries and crypto wallets, exfiltrated to attacker infrastructure via Telegram; Microsoft and Apple have pushed detection updates after responsible disclosure.

incidents.vector: Five custom backdoors and loaders deployed on victim systems, each abusing a different mainstream cloud service (Slack, Discord, Microsoft Outlook drafts, file.io) for command-and-control and file exfiltration.
incidents.impact: ESET researchers found 12 backdoored systems inside one Mongolian government institution and assess dozens more victims have likely been affected; activity dating back to at least 2023.

incidents.vector: Operators social-engineered an employee and used legitimate purchased credentials to log in, then abused authorised account permissions to delete/disable assets — no novel malware or zero-day involved.
incidents.impact: Thousands of medical devices were reported disabled; described publicly as a high-profile retaliatory attack but characterised by former NSA leadership as a 'low-and-slow' identity-led intrusion amplified by an information-operation narrative.

incidents.vector: Data was originally lifted from Hallmark's Salesforce environment during a wave of token/voice-phishing-style cloud account compromises last year; leaked publicly after a ransom demand was refused.
incidents.impact: Personal information of approximately 1.7 million Hallmark customers posted on a criminal leak site.

incidents.victim: U.S. federal civilian agency (unnamed) and other Cisco ASA/Firepower owners
incidents.vector: Attackers exploited the September 2025 Cisco ASA WebVPN flaws (CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362), then implanted FIRESTARTER, a backdoor that hides in firewall firmware by manipulating the Cisco Service Platform mount list to survive software reboots and patching, plus a second implant 'Line Viper' that creates unauthenticated VPN sessions and steals credentials and keys.
incidents.impact: CISA's monitoring caught the implant on a federal civilian agency Firepower device that had been compromised since before September 25, 2025 and re-accessed in March 2026; CISA updated Emergency Directive 25-03 and ordered all FCEB agencies to inventory Cisco firewalls, collect memory snapshots, and physically power-cycle infected devices. Joint advisory issued with NCSC-UK; broad range of Firepower 1000/2100/4100/9300 and Secure Firewall 1200/3100/4200 series affected.

incidents.vector: Initial foothold came from a Context.ai (third-party AI tool) employee whose laptop was infected with Lumma Stealer after searching for Roblox cheats; stolen OAuth tokens then granted access to Vercel's internal systems where attackers enumerated environment variables and pulled keys, source code and customer data.
incidents.impact: Vercel says a 'small number' of customers were impacted but expanded the scope after analysing nearly a petabyte of logs; criminals (claiming the ShinyHunters brand, possibly an imposter) are advertising stolen access keys, source code and databases for sale. Mandiant is leading the response; downstream customers urged to rotate credentials.

incidents.victim: Bitwarden CLI users via npm and Checkmarx tooling users
incidents.vector: Compromised developer credentials at security vendor Checkmarx were used to publish a malicious @bitwarden/cli npm package, poisoned Docker Hub images (checkmarx/kics), a backdoored GitHub Actions workflow and trojanised VS Code extensions; the npm preinstall script downloads Bun runtime, harvests cloud, npm and GitHub tokens, and republishes any other npm package the victim can publish (a self-propagating 'Shai-Hulud' worm pattern).
incidents.impact: Latest wave in a multi-month supply-chain campaign that has hit Trivy, LiteLLM and now Checkmarx distribution channels; affected developers can lose AWS, GCP, Azure, npm and GitHub credentials and have downstream packages weaponised against their organisations. Unit 42 publishing detections; widespread Checkmarx customer impact.

incidents.victim: Cambodia-based scam-compound network targeting U.S. victims
incidents.vector: Pig-butchering style romance/investment scams run from compounds protected by Cambodian Senator Kok An, with trafficked workers coerced into running social engineering and fake crypto investment platforms.
incidents.impact: OFAC sanctioned Senator Kok An plus 28 individuals/entities; Scam Center Strike Force seized 503 fraudulent crypto-investment domains and a messaging app used to recruit trafficking victims; charges filed against two operators of a Burma compound; State offering up to $10M reward for related crypto-fraud proceeds. USSS previously tied this network to over $73M laundered from U.S. victims.

incidents.victim: Government and email-provider users on 18,000+ small-office routers (multiple countries)
incidents.vector: GRU operators exploited known flaws in end-of-life Mikrotik and TP-Link SOHO routers to silently rewrite each router's DNS settings to attacker-controlled servers, then used those DNS records to intercept Microsoft 365 OAuth authentication tokens — no malware deployed on user endpoints.
incidents.impact: Black Lotus Labs/Lumen and Microsoft identified more than 200 organisations and around 5,000 consumer devices caught in the dragnet, which peaked in December 2025 and primarily targeted ministries of foreign affairs, law-enforcement bodies and third-party email providers; NCSC-UK published a coordinated advisory on the technique.

incidents.vector: Unspecified cyberattack on Friday April 17 prompted a precautionary network shutdown — language used (Leon County 'disconnecting to prevent contamination') is consistent with a ransomware response.
incidents.impact: City IT network and several public-facing services taken offline for days; surrounding Leon County severed its connection from the city to limit exposure.

incidents.victim: Israeli national water and desalination operators (attempted)
incidents.vector: ZionSiphon OT-aware malware checks for Israeli IP ranges and hard-coded names of Israeli water and desalination companies, then targets specific OT directories and pushes pump pressure and chlorine levels to maximum settings.
incidents.impact: Darktrace researchers found the sample, which contains pro-Iran/anti-Israel strings ('Poisoning the population of Tel Aviv and Haifa'); a XOR encoding bug breaks the IP-range check, causing the malware to delete itself before activating, so no real-world impact has been confirmed.

incidents.vector: Attacker exploited a vulnerability in the Kelp DAO restaking platform to mint and withdraw rsETH (restaked Ether) tokens.
incidents.impact: Approximately $292 million in crypto assets drained — the largest cryptocurrency theft of the year so far.

incidents.vector: Attackers spent days seeding fake token pools and then triggered vulnerabilities in the protocol's margin trading feature to drain assets through the manipulated pools.
Unspecified intrusion exposing reservation and customer-profile data; pattern is consistent with prior travel-platform credential compromises.
incidents.impact: Booking details, names, emails, home addresses and phone numbers exposed; no financial data taken; the company reset reservation PINs for affected customers.

incidents.vector: Sustained, sophisticated distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) traffic against Bluesky's infrastructure; further DDoS attempts continued through the week.
incidents.impact: Bluesky operated intermittently for nearly a day on April 15-16 before mitigations stabilised; subsequent attacks failed to take the site down again.

incidents.victim: German Mittelstand companies (legal services, manufacturing, construction, retail)
incidents.vector: Mid-tier ransomware crews (notably the operations branded SafePay and Qilin) targeting small and mid-sized German firms — 96% of 2025 German leak victims had under 5,000 employees — using AI-aided localisation to overcome the language barrier and pressuring victims via data-leak-site posts when ransoms are refused.
incidents.impact: Google Threat Intelligence reports German data-leak-site posts grew 92% in 2025, three times the European average; SafePay alone claimed 76 German victims (25% of country total) and Qilin had already posted 13 German victims by early 2026, making Germany Europe's top extortion target.

incidents.vector: Improper input validation flaw (CVE-2026-32201) in Microsoft Office SharePoint allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to spoof trusted SharePoint content over the network; exploited as a zero-day before patches shipped.
incidents.impact: Microsoft confirmed active in-the-wild exploitation in April Patch Tuesday; attackers can present falsified content inside trusted SharePoint environments to enable phishing, data manipulation and follow-on social engineering. Patch is now available; CrowdStrike, Krebs and SANS all flag urgent remediation. April release also included BlueHammer Defender bug (CVE-2026-33825) and an actively exploited Adobe Reader zero-day (CVE-2026-34621).
